excel_automation.pattern:
Dim xlApp As Object
Dim xlWorkbook As Object
Dim xlWorksheet As Object

Set xlApp = CreateObject("Excel.Application")
xlApp.Visible = True
Set xlWorkbook = xlApp.Workbooks.Add
Set xlWorksheet = xlWorkbook.Worksheets(1)
xlWorksheet.Cells(1, 1).Value = "Hello Excel"
xlWorkbook.SaveAs "C:\test.xlsx"
xlApp.Quit
Set xlApp = Nothing

word_automation.pattern:
Dim wdApp As Object
Dim wdDoc As Object

Set wdApp = CreateObject("Word.Application")
wdApp.Visible = True
Set wdDoc = wdApp.Documents.Add
wdDoc.Range.Text = "Hello Word"
wdDoc.SaveAs "C:\test.docx"
wdApp.Quit
Set wdApp = Nothing

filesystem_object.pattern:
Dim fso As Object
Set fso = CreateObject("Scripting.FileSystemObject")

If fso.FileExists("C:\test.txt") Then
    Set file = fso.OpenTextFile("C:\test.txt", 1)
    content = file.ReadAll
    file.Close
End If

Set folder = fso.GetFolder("C:\")
For Each file In folder.Files
    Debug.Print file.Name
Next

wscript_shell.pattern:
Dim shell As Object
Set shell = CreateObject("WScript.Shell")

' Run external program
shell.Run "notepad.exe", 1, True

' Read registry
regValue = shell.RegRead("HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ProgramFilesDir")

' Environment variables
envValue = shell.ExpandEnvironmentStrings("%TEMP%")

activex_control.pattern:
' In VB6 Form designer, add ActiveX control
' Then in code:
Private Sub WebBrowser1_NavigateComplete2(ByVal pDisp As Object, URL As Variant)
    Debug.Print "Navigated to: " & URL
End Sub

WebBrowser1.Navigate "http://www.example.com"

com_dll_late_binding.pattern:
Dim obj As Object
Set obj = CreateObject("MyCustom.Application")
result = obj.DoSomething("parameter")
Set obj = Nothing
com_dll_late_binding.description: Late binding COM object creation and method invocation

com_dll_early_binding.pattern:
' Reference added to COM library in VB6
Dim obj As MyCustomLibrary.Application
Set obj = New MyCustomLibrary.Application
result = obj.DoSomething("parameter")
Set obj = Nothing
com_dll_early_binding.description: Early binding COM objects with type libraries

ado_connection.pattern:
Dim conn As ADODB.Connection
Set conn = CreateObject("ADODB.Connection")
conn.ConnectionString = "Provider=SQLOLEDB;Server=localhost;Database=TestDB;Trusted_Connection=yes"
conn.Open
ado_connection.description: ADODB Connection replaced with modern data access

scripting_dictionary.pattern:
Dim dict As Object
Set dict = CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary")
dict.Add "key1", "value1"
dict.Add "key2", "value2"

If dict.Exists("key1") Then
    value = dict.Item("key1")
End If

For Each key In dict.Keys
    Debug.Print key & " = " & dict.Item(key)
Next

xml_dom.pattern:
Dim xmlDoc As Object
Set xmlDoc = CreateObject("MSXML2.DOMDocument")
xmlDoc.LoadXML "<root><item>value</item></root>"

Set node = xmlDoc.SelectSingleNode("//item")
If Not node Is Nothing Then
    Debug.Print node.Text
End If
xml_dom.description: MSXML DOM replaced with System.Xml or LINQ to XML

http_request.pattern:
Dim http As Object
Set http = CreateObject("MSXML2.XMLHTTP")
http.Open "GET", "http://api.example.com/data", False
http.Send

If http.Status = 200 Then
    response = http.ResponseText
    Debug.Print response
End If
http_request.description: XMLHTTP requests replaced with HttpClient or WebClient

com_error_handling.pattern:
On Error Resume Next
Set obj = CreateObject("NonExistent.Application")
If Err.Number <> 0 Then
    MsgBox "COM Error: " & Err.Description
    Err.Clear
End If
On Error GoTo 0
com_error_handling.description: COM error handling with try-catch and COMException

com_event_handling.pattern:
' In VB6 class module
Private WithEvents xlApp As Excel.Application

Private Sub xlApp_WorkbookOpen(ByVal Wb As Excel.Workbook)
    Debug.Print "Workbook opened: " & Wb.Name
End Sub

' In main code
Set xlApp = New Excel.Application

com_cleanup.pattern:
Set obj = Nothing
Set xlApp = Nothing
Set wdApp = Nothing

' VB6 automatically handled COM cleanup
com_cleanup.description: Proper COM object cleanup and memory management

registry_operations.pattern:
Dim shell As Object
Set shell = CreateObject("WScript.Shell")

' Read registry value
value = shell.RegRead("HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\MyApp\Setting")

' Write registry value
shell.RegWrite "HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\MyApp\Setting", "NewValue", "REG_SZ"

' Delete registry key
shell.RegDelete "HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\MyApp\OldSetting"
